title: pycnandra cylindricarpa
intro: Pycnandra cylindricarpa, also known as the Kava of the Pacific, is a plant species native to the Pacific islands, specifically found in the island nations of Vanuatu and New Caledonia. It is a climbing woody vine with medicinal properties, traditionally used for its analgesic, anxiolytic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant's roots are rich in kavalactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, providing relief from anxiety, insomnia, and pain, as well as other health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving mood. In traditional medicine, the roots are often prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be used in capsule or powder form for consumption.
